sprint: Sprint 31 -- V0.27.5 Chain-merge prep (/chain-validated slash command + Drive 11+ forensic instrumentation)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #4 in the V0.27 chain (NOT a literal bug-fix sprint; preparation work for the eventual chain merge to main). Per CIO 2026-05-10 Session 31 directive: V0.27.5 builds B-067 /chain-validated slash command (Option A pick) + adds B-071 Drive 11+ end-to-end forensic instrumentation (diagnostics half of CIO bracket 'non-drive-required bug fixes OR drive-required diagnostics'). Both stories are non-Drive-blocked; B-067 builds workflow infra; B-071 adds INFO log lines so Drive 11+ produces complete evidence in one drive. Per CIO chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.5 branches FROM sprint/sprint30-bugfixes-V0.27.4 (cumulative chain), NOT from main.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.4 baseline (V0.27.4 sprint-tip @ a46ba0a). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Prep work for the eventual V0.27 chain merge to main. Two parallel stories: US-318 builds the /chain-validated slash command (B-067; workflow infra consummating the chain-end-merge ritual); US-319 adds INFO log instrumentation across DriveDetector + drive_summary writers + sync UPDATE propagation + drive_counter advance (B-071; diagnostics half of CIO bracket so Drive 11+ produces complete IRL validation evidence in ONE drive).
EXECUTION ORDER (parallel): TIER 1 (parallel) -- US-318 /chain-validated slash command (M, P1) + US-319 Drive 11+ forensic instrumentation (S, P2). Independent stories; no inter-dependency.

THEME: 'Ready the rails for chain merge + the drive.' V0.27 chain is on stacked sprint branches; B-063 fuse-box wiring + Drive 11+ are the gate to validate the chain. While that hardware blocker sits with CIO, V0.27.5 makes the eventual unblock-and-validate cycle as efficient as possible: one drive captures everything (via B-071 forensic logs), one slash command merges the chain (via B-067 /chain-validated). No bug fixes in this sprint (bug queue is empty post-V0.27.4); pure infra + observability.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-30 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) BL-010 + verify-table-names rule applied. (d) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM verified the underlying premises (V0.27 chain branches state, sync_log INSERT-monotone behavior) hold for V0.27.5 work. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran rg src/pi/obdii/drive/ + src/pi/sync/ during grooming to identify instrumentation target surfaces; baked into US-319 scope. (f) feedback_pm_main_merges_at_chain_end_only.md: V0.27.5 STAYS on sprint branch; main waits for whole V0.27 chain fully functional working. (g) feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md: any new CLI surfaces in /chain-validated MUST be validated from CIO's actual shell context (Windows, no env vars set).

GROUNDING SOURCES: CIO 2026-05-10 Session 31 directive 'go with option A and add any non drive required bug fixes or drive required diagnostics' -- B-067 covers Option A; B-071 covers diagnostics. Plus V0.27.2 US-307 forensic instrumentation precedent (BL-012 Option A pattern) + V0.27.4 chain-tip state (V0.27 chain on stacked sprint branches awaiting Drive 11+ + B-063).

POST-SPRINT-31 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes B-063 fuse-box wiring (gates Drive 11+). (2) Drive 11+ post-fuse-box first clean car-coupled drive; B-071 instrumentation makes the evidence comprehensive. (3) B-066 B-047 self-update IRL drill (CIO + PM cooperative; gates chain merge). (4) Once V0.27 chain fully validated, CIO invokes /chain-validated (built in US-318) to merge V0.27 chain to main as new fully-functional-working stable.

stories.title: /chain-validated slash command (B-067 -- V0.27 chain merge ritual)
stories.intent: B-067: per CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge rule, V0.27 chain merges to main when whole chain fully functional working. Currently no slash command exists for this ritual -- manual git ritual is 6+ commands + 5+ file edits + risk of divergence between merge state + manifest state. V0.27.5 builds /chain-validated slash command + supporting Python script(s) for the deterministic operations. Future chain merges (V0.28, V0.29, ...) inherit the same workflow.

filesToTouch: .claude/commands/chain-validated.md (NEW -- slash command file with 8 phases: pre-flight gates / aggregate chain status / confirm chain-tip 'fully functional working' / update manifest chain-wide / merge chain to main / tag new stable / update PM artifacts on main / final summary)

offices/pm/scripts/chain_validate_aggregate.py (NEW -- supporting Python script: enumerate sprint branches in chain; aggregate validatesFeatures across all chain sprints; print chain-wide bigDoD checklist; assert no NEVER-validated features in chain scope)

offices/pm/scripts/chain_validate_manifest_bump.py (NEW -- supporting Python script: for each feature in any chain sprint's validatesFeatures, bump lastValidated to chain-merge date + mark 'by chain merge V0.X.N' in validatedBy)
offices/pm/scripts/README.md (UPDATE -- add chain_validate_aggregate.py + chain_validate_manifest_bump.py to the script-call graph table; document the V0.27 chain merge as canonical first invocation)

tests/pm_scripts/test_chain_validate_aggregate.py (NEW -- mocks a synthetic test-branch chain; asserts script enumerates branches correctly + aggregates validatesFeatures + detects NEVER-validated features)
filesToRead: offices/pm/backlog/B-067-chain-validated-slash-command.md (full spec with 8-phase proposal + naming alternative + sprint timing)
.claude/commands/sprint-deploy-pm.md (deploy ritual; chain-validated is its sister command)
offices/pm/scripts/pm_regression_status.py (existing manifest query script; chain_validate_aggregate.py extends similar patterns)
offices/pm/regression_manifest.json (schema; chain merge bumps lastValidated for whole chain's validatesFeatures aggregate)
doNotTouch: Per-sprint /sprint-validated semantics (still bumps manifest + marks sprint validated; just does NOT merge under chain-end-merge rule)
regression_manifest.json schema (only bumping lastValidated values; not adding/removing fields)

groundingRefs.value: CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge rule: main = fully functional working system; V0.27 chain merges to main when whole chain clean

Manual chain-merge ritual is error-prone: 5+ sprint branches to consolidate, RELEASE_VERSION to bump, manifest entries to bump, deploy stamps to align, regression-status reports to verify. Slash command makes ritual repeatable for V0.28+ chains too

Real-world validation gate: smoke-tested against a synthetic test branch chain that exercises merge logic without affecting real branches; output asserts chain-tip merges + tag pushes + main bumps to chain-tip version

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: ls .claude/commands/ + rg `git merge|git tag|chain` offices/pm/scripts/ -- map existing slash commands + Python scripts; identify reusable patterns (verify_release_version + bump_passed_statuses + archive_sprint_artifacts as precedents)
/chain-validated slash command file at .claude/commands/chain-validated.md with 8 phases documented + stop-condition flowchart + post-execution summary table
chain_validate_aggregate.py + chain_validate_manifest_bump.py support scripts in offices/pm/scripts/; idempotent + stdlib-only + CLI-first
tests/pm_scripts/test_chain_validate_aggregate.py: synthetic chain coverage; would FAIL pre-fix (no such script exists)
verification: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q (existing tests stay GREEN)
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS pre-fix (no chain-validate scripts exist)

Real-world validation gate: SMOKE-tested against synthetic chain BEFORE invoked on real V0.27 chain. /chain-validated has stop-conditions that abort on any failure-mode (incomplete chain validation, uncommitted state, divergent branches)
Per-sprint /sprint-validated semantics unchanged (still bumps manifest + marks sprint validated)
Idempotent: safe to re-run if interrupted between phases (each phase has explicit pre-flight + commit-marker semantics)
stopConditions: If pre-flight audit reveals existing slash commands have a structural pattern incompatible with multi-branch operations (e.g., explicit single-branch assumptions) -- STOP, document; propose minimum-rework approach
If supporting scripts surface circular-import or other Python packaging issues -- STOP, file architectural-ambiguity blocker
If smoke test against synthetic chain reveals git merge edge case (octopus merge needed? rebase preferred?) -- STOP, document; PM evaluates merge strategy

stories.title: Drive 11+ end-to-end forensic instrumentation (B-071)
stories.intent: B-071: per CIO 2026-05-10 Session 31 directive 'add any non drive required bug fixes or drive required diagnostics' -- this story is the diagnostics half. V0.27 chain has 8 IRL bigDoD clauses pending; without instrumentation, Drive 11+ likely needs 2-3 follow-up drives to chase missing logs. Add INFO log instrumentation at four surfaces: DriveDetector state transitions (I-019/US-311 hypothesis discrimination) + drive_summary writer call sites (US-310/US-317 validation) + sync UPDATE propagation per-table (US-315/B-065 validation) + drive_counter advance + sync push (US-314 watch-item resolution). Same pattern as V0.27.2 US-307 forensic instrumentation -- pure observability, no behavior change.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py (UPDATE -- add INFO log lines at _checkDriveStart + _checkDriveEnd entry/exit + KEY_OFF<->ACTIVE transitions; logs RPM + SPEED + DriveState + MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS check result + drive_id when assigned; ~10 LOC; behavior unchanged)
src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py (UPDATE -- add INFO log line at Pi-side drive_summary INSERT/UPDATE callsites; log drive_id + which fields about to write; ~3 LOC)
src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- add INFO log line at _ensureDriveSummary entry server-side; log drive_id + 12-field write intent + pingOllama state; ~3 LOC)
src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE -- add INFO log at sync UPDATE propagation per-table per US-315 modified_at cursor advance; ~5 LOC)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_drive_11_forensic_logging.py (NEW -- synthetic drive_start -> drive_end + sync cycle; asserts INFO log lines covering the 4 target surfaces; would FAIL pre-fix because the log lines don't exist)
